ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR APPLICATION TO ADMIT ATTORNEY MARSHALL S. TURNER PRO HAC VICE HEARING SET FOR SEP-05-2019 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE VERIFIED APPLICATION OF MARSHALL S. TURNER TO APPEAR PRO HAC VICE AS COUNSEL TO PLAINTIFF MENZIES AVIATION (USA) INC. IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED, AND THE APPLICATION CONFORMS TO CAL. R. CT. 9.40. THE COURT DENIES THE REQUEST THAT IT ORDER THAT MR. TURNER NEED NOT BE SERVED WITH PAPERS IN THIS CASE; THE PARTIES ARE FREE TO REACH THEIR OWN AGREEMENT AS TO SERVICE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

Off Calendar Aug-30-2019 Continued To Oct-23-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion For Stay Pending Resolution Of Underlying Case.
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Motion For Stay Pending Resolution Of Underlying Case.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for stay pending resolution of underlying case is granted. "To eliminate the risk of inconsistent factual determinations that could prejudice the insured, a stay of the declaratory relief action pending resolution of the third party suit is appropriate when the coverage question turns on facts to be litigated in the underlying action." (Montrose Chemical Corp. v. Superior Court (1993) 6 Cal.4th 287, 301.)

Although the classic case under Montrose is a situation where an insurer can undermine the defense of its insured in an underlying litigation by attacking it on the merits, Montrose is not limited to such a scenario.
"If the factual issues to be resolved in the declaratory relief action overlap with issues to be resolved in the underlying litigation, the trial court must stay the declaratory relief action." (Great American Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 221, 235.)

"A court considering whether to stay a declaratory relief action must therefore consider precisely which issues are to be litigated in order to resolve the declaratory relief action, and whether those issues are related to factual issues yet to be litigated in the underlying action." (Id.) Here, Plaintiffs' attempt to distinguish the factual issues to be litigated in this action for declaratory relief and in the underlying Tran and Lu v. Javelin et al. action is unpersuasive. In Tran, the claim against Menzies is that it "negligently owned, managed, leased, controlled, and/or operated the land" and "traffic at and around the area" where Mr. Tran was struck. (First Amended Compl., 85.) CCSF's cross-complaint against Menzies for indemnification and defense similarly alleges that Menzies "negligently and/or carelessly conducted" itself in its operations at the airport, leading to Mr. Tran's accident. (Cross-Compl., 7-8.) Moreover, in this action for declaratory relief, Plaintiffs argue that they do not owe the City a defense or indemnification because the Tran incident was not caused by any act or omission by Menzies, Menzies' operations at the airport, or any default by Menzies. Ultimately, the key factual issue in these matters is the same: whether Menzies caused, through its actions or inactions, the Tran accident, or if Menzies is otherwise responsible for that accident. Thus, contrary to Menzies' contention, the declaratory relief action and the Tran action raise overlapping issues, and Menzies does not show that the instant action can be resolved without resolving any fact of consequence in the underlying action.

As such, a stay of Plaintiffs' action for declaratory relief is proper and mandatory.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)

Application To Admit Attorney Marshall S. Turner Pro Hac Vice
Off Calendar Sep-05-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: Atty. ANDREW C. JOHNSON, ESQ. of Condon & Forsyth, LLP (Tel: 410-557-2030) appearing via CourtCall for Menzies Aviation (USA) Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Application To Admit Attorney Marshall S. Turner Pro Hac Vice.

No tentative ruling was issued in this matter as the matter was erroneously not set on Dept. 302's September 5, 2019 calendar. The Court having received and reviewed the moving party's papers, the Court now rules as follows: The verified application of Marshall S. Turner to appear pro hac vice as counsel to plaintiff Menzies Aviation (USA) Inc. is granted. No opposition filed, and the application conforms to Cal. R. Ct. 9.40. The Court denies the request that it order that Mr. Turner need not be served with papers in this case; the parties are free to reach their own agreement as to service.

Order signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EPS)
